gwasplot 0.0.0.9000
  • Get started
  • Reference

Extract the top GWAS markers as a tidy table

Source: R/table.R
gwas_top.Rd

Returns the top hits selected by a highlight_top() specification, ordered by p-value, with a neg_log10_p column added. This is the data behind gwas_table() and uses the same selection logic as the plots' highlight argument, so a table and a figure can be kept perfectly in sync.

Usage

gwas_top(data, highlight = highlight_top(top_n = 10), columns = NULL)

Arguments

data

A GWAS data frame (passed through validate_gwas()).

highlight

A highlight_top() spec, or a single number (top_n). Defaults to the 10 smallest-p markers.

columns

Optional character vector of columns to keep (and order). By default keeps SNP, gene, trait, CHR, POS, P, neg_log10_p when present.

Value

A tibble of the selected markers.

See also

gwas_table(), highlight_top()

Examples

data(gwas_example)
gwas_top(gwas_example, highlight = highlight_top(top_n = 5))
#> # A tibble: 5 × 6
#>   SNP    gene      CHR        POS        P neg_log10_p
#>   <chr>  <chr>     <fct>    <dbl>    <dbl>       <dbl>
#> 1 rs5486 GENE20370 20     1342370 2.55e-13        12.6
#> 2 rs5485 GENE20950 20      651950 5.50e-13        12.3
#> 3 rs5487 GENE20702 20     1501702 5.50e-13        12.3
#> 4 rs646  GENE02691 2     65881691 7.94e-13        12.1
#> 5 rs5484 GENE20446 20      535446 1.18e-12        11.9
